finding: Same-repo `taskflow close` could be blocked by unrelated active planning-mirror / direction-lock findings because the pre-commit protected-state checks evaluated all active direction locks instead of only the locks relevant to the staged governed bundle.
expectedBehavior: Pre-commit scope checks for taskflow close should filter active direction locks down to the staged bundle so foreign planning mirrors do not raise false `ATM_PLANNING_MIRROR_DRIFT` or `ATM_TASK_DIRECTION_SCOPE_DRIFT` failures.
evidenceOrRepro: Verified on 2026-06-28 that the source fix is present in `packages/cli/src/commands/hook.ts` via `selectRelevantDirectionLocksForCommit(...)`, and the focused same-repo close regression passes in `packages/cli/src/commands/__tests__/framework-mode-staged-residue.spec.ts`. This repair lane promoted that regression into a first-class validator surface through `scripts/validate-framework-hook-scope-filtering.ts`, `package.json`, and `scripts/validators.config.json`, so standard ATM validation now guards the foreign-planning-lock case instead of leaving it as an ad hoc test file. Validation used `node --strip-types packages/cli/src/commands/__tests__/framework-mode-staged-residue.spec.ts`, `npm run validate:framework-hook-scope-filtering`, and post-build frozen release verification.
followUp: Keep `selectRelevantDirectionLocksForCommit(...)` as the common staged-bundle filter for direction-lock scope checks, and preserve the new validator in standard profiles so same-repo close bundles cannot regress back to foreign planning-lock false positives.
